response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack web engineer with deep JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang expertise, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2007–2008) and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. There is no evidence of fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets systems work; RIDE Capital was an early-stage fintech startup, not a capital-markets environment. SQL Server and Oracle are not mentioned, and the overall skill set is oriented toward web services, cloud infrastructure, and developer tooling rather than low-latency financial systems.
strengths: Long career spanning 20+ years shows adaptability and sustained technical growth across diverse domains
Some early C++ and C exposure at National Instruments, plus low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O), hints at systems-level intuition
CI/CD, Docker, and infrastructure experience aligns with optional desirable skills around pipelines and tooling
concerns: C++ professional experience is negligible (~6 months in 2007–2008), far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge present in the entire resume
No mention of Windows platform development, SQL Server, Oracle, STL, Python, Kafka, or any other mandatory or high-priority optional skills for this role
